sanskrit:
भक्त्या त्वनन्यया शक्य अहमेवंविधोऽर्जुन।
ज्ञातुं द्रष्टुं च तत्त्वेन प्रवेष्टुं च परन्तप॥
transliteration:
bhaktyā tv ananyayā śakya aham evaṃ-vidho ’rjuna |
jñātuṃ draṣṭuṃ ca tattvena praveṣṭuṃ ca parantapa ||
literal_meaning: By unalloyed devotion alone I, in this very form, can be known, seen in truth, and entered into, O Parantapa.
contextual_meaning: After the terrifying vision of universal destruction, Arjuna is told that genuine knowing—cognitive, perceptual, and existential—of the Divine is possible only through single-pointed love that is not diluted by bargaining (karma) or analysis (jñāna).
psychological_state: Post-traumatic openness: the ego, shattered by cosmic dread, now seeks re-anchoring in relational devotion rather than intellectual mastery.
trigger_scenario: A client who has faced mortality through ICU delirium realizes that neither data nor good deeds calm the dread; only unconditional love for something beyond the self restores safety.
anchor_text: Know Me through wholehearted love, not rituals or theories.
